questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What must you do when you approach a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down but keep moving if the way is clear
Come to a complete stop and remain stopped until it turns green
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Treat it the same as a yield sign
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires a full stop, and you may not proceed until the light turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Stop and wait for a green light
questions.explanation: A steady yellow signal warns that the light is about to turn red, so you should stop unless you are too close to stop safely.

questions.prompt: What shape and color is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red octagon, a unique shape so it can be recognized even when partly covered.

questions.prompt: In West Virginia, when may you turn right at a steady red light?
choices: After a complete stop, yielding to pedestrians and cross traffic, unless a sign prohibits it
Only if there is a green arrow
Without stopping if no one is around
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed after you stop completely and yield, unless a posted sign forbids the turn.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a flashing red traffic signal?
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe, as you would at a stop sign
Treat it like a steady green light
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop, yield, then go when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road mean?
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited in that direction.

questions.prompt: What does an orange traffic sign usually indicate?
choices: A construction or maintenance work zone
questions.explanation: Orange signs warn drivers of construction and maintenance zones where extra caution is required.

questions.prompt: What is the general meaning of a yellow warning sign?
questions.explanation: Yellow signs warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead, such as curves, intersections, or crossings.

questions.prompt: What do white lane lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
The road from the shoulder only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction.

questions.prompt: What does a green traffic light mean?
choices: You may proceed if the intersection is clear
Speed up to beat the next light
Yield to all vehicles behind you
questions.explanation: A green light means you may go, but only after making sure the intersection is clear of crossing traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who has the right-of-way when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, who must you yield to?
choices: Oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must yield to oncoming vehicles that are close enough to pose a danger.

questions.prompt: What must you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with its siren and lights on?
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop until it passes
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they have passed.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When entering a roadway from a private driveway, what should you do?
choices: Proceed first because you were there first
Stop and yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the road
questions.explanation: A driver entering from a driveway or private road must yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the roadway.

questions.prompt: From which lane should you begin a left turn on a two-way street?
choices: The lane nearest the right curb
The lane nearest the center line
questions.explanation: Left turns should begin from the lane closest to the center line.

questions.prompt: At a roundabout, what must you do before entering?
choices: Stop completely even if it is clear
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to vehicles already going around it and enter only when there is a safe gap.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal in West Virginia?
choices: Only as you begin the turn
questions.explanation: You should signal your intention at least 100 feet before making a turn to warn other drivers.

questions.prompt: Who must yield when a vehicle approaches a yield sign?
choices: The driver facing the yield sign must yield
questions.explanation: A yield sign requires the driver facing it to slow down and give the right-of-way to traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: You must always yield to a pedestrian who is using which of the following?
choices: A white cane or guide dog
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog, as they may have limited vision.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the speed limit in a West Virginia business or residential district?
questions.explanation: The default speed limit in business and residential districts is 25 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: What is the typical maximum speed limit on a rural West Virginia interstate highway?
questions.explanation: Rural interstate highways in West Virginia generally have a maximum speed limit of 70 mph.

questions.prompt: What does the Basic Speed Law require of drivers?
choices: Always drive the posted limit exactly
Never drive faster than is reasonable and safe for conditions
Drive 5 mph below the limit at all times
Match the speed of surrounding traffic
questions.explanation: The Basic Speed Law means you must adjust your speed to conditions and never go faster than is safe, even within the posted limit.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum following distance recommended in good conditions?
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you a safe following distance in good conditions and time to react.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit on an open country highway in West Virginia unless otherwise posted?
questions.explanation: Open country highways have a default speed limit of 55 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your following distance in rain or fog?
choices: Increase it to four seconds or more
questions.explanation: In poor conditions you should increase your following distance to four seconds or more because stopping takes longer.

questions.prompt: Why is tailgating dangerous?
choices: It leaves no room to stop safely and causes rear-end crashes
It is required in heavy traffic
questions.explanation: Following too closely leaves no space to stop and is a leading cause of rear-end collisions.

questions.prompt: When should you slow below the posted speed limit?
choices: Never; the limit is always safe
In rain, fog, snow, heavy traffic, or near schools and work zones
questions.explanation: You should reduce speed below the posted limit whenever conditions make the posted speed unsafe.

questions.prompt: How can you help drivers behind you stop safely?
questions.explanation: Braking early and gradually gives drivers behind you time to react and reduces the risk of a rear-end crash.

questions.prompt: When is it illegal to pass another vehicle?
choices: When a solid yellow line is on your side of the road
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, which lane should you generally keep to except when passing?
questions.explanation: Keep right except when passing, and use the left lane mainly for passing slower traffic.

questions.prompt: What should you do if another driver is passing you?
choices: Maintain or slightly reduce your speed and stay in your lane
questions.explanation: When being passed you should keep your speed steady or slow slightly so the other driver can pass safely.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid passing near a hill or curve?
choices: It is bad for fuel economy
You cannot see far enough ahead to pass safely
It is allowed only at night
questions.explanation: On hills and curves you cannot see oncoming traffic far enough ahead, so passing is unsafe and usually illegal.

questions.prompt: What should you do before changing lanes?
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot
questions.explanation: Always signal, check your mirrors, and look over your shoulder to check the blind spot before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: How close to an intersection is passing generally prohibited?
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing for safety.

questions.prompt: What does a single broken white line between two same-direction lanes mean?
choices: You may change lanes when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken white line allows lane changes when it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when should you return to your lane?
choices: Only after you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Return to your lane only when you can see the vehicle you passed in your rearview mirror, leaving it safe space.

questions.prompt: How much space should you leave when passing a bicyclist in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: Drivers must leave at least three feet of space when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Under West Virginia's Move Over law, what must you do on a divided highway when you cannot change lanes away from a stopped emergency vehicle?
choices: Slow to 25 mph and pass with caution
questions.explanation: If you cannot safely move over on a divided highway, you must slow to 25 mph and pass carefully.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
choices: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and easily hidden in blind spots, so you must check carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: What is true about the blind spots of large trucks?
choices: If you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you
Blind spots are only behind the truck
Trucks can see all around them
questions.explanation: Large trucks have big blind spots; if you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: Under the Move Over law, what should you do on an undivided road when passing a stopped vehicle with warning lights?
questions.explanation: On an undivided road you must slow to 15 mph when passing a stopped emergency or warning-light vehicle.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid cutting in front of a large truck and then slowing down?
choices: Trucks need much more distance to stop and cannot brake quickly
questions.explanation: Large trucks need far more distance to stop, so cutting in and slowing down can cause a serious rear-end crash.

questions.prompt: How much room should you give a motorcycle in traffic?
choices: Enough to share the lane beside it
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, so never crowd or share a lane with one.

questions.prompt: When you see slow-moving farm equipment ahead on a rural road, what should you do?
choices: Slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly, so slow down and pass only where it is safe and passing is permitted.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are considered legally impaired at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: What is the zero-tolerance BAC limit for drivers under 21 in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: Under West Virginia's zero-tolerance law, drivers under 21 may not drive with a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Is texting while driving legal in West Virginia?
choices: No, it is banned and is a primary offense
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is banned for all drivers and is a primary offense, meaning an officer can stop you for it alone.

questions.prompt: What happens if you refuse a chemical test for alcohol in West Virginia?
choices: You must retake the road test
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing a chemical test results in an automatic license suspension separate from any DUI charge.

questions.prompt: What is the fine for a first handheld phone or texting offense in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: The fine for a first handheld phone or texting violation is $100, rising for repeat offenses.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment and coordination
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time and reduces judgment and coordination, making driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive?
choices: Illegal drugs as well as some prescription and over-the-counter medicines
questions.explanation: Many legal medications, not just illegal drugs, can impair you enough to make driving unsafe and unlawful.

questions.prompt: What is the BAC limit for a commercial vehicle driver in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers are held to a stricter BAC limit of 0.04 percent.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to avoid distracted driving?
choices: Keep your phone in your lap
Keep your eyes on the road, hands on the wheel, and mind on driving
Eat while driving to save time
questions.explanation: Avoiding distractions means keeping your eyes on the road, hands on the wheel, and attention on driving.

questions.prompt: In West Virginia, when are you required to turn on your headlights?
choices: Whenever your windshield wipers are in use
questions.explanation: West Virginia law requires headlights whenever your wipers are in use and from sunset to sunrise.

questions.prompt: What type of headlights should you use in fog?
questions.explanation: Use low beams in fog because high beams reflect off the fog and reduce your visibility.

questions.prompt: When is a road often most slippery?
choices: After it has rained for hours
At the start of a rain when water mixes with oil
questions.explanation: Roads are most slippery when rain first begins and mixes with oil and dust on the surface.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and keep the steering wheel steady
questions.explanation: If you hydroplane, ease off the accelerator and hold the wheel steady until your tires regain traction; do not brake hard.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road freezes before others in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other roads because cold air surrounds them on all sides.

questions.prompt: If your car begins to skid, what should you do?
choices: Brake hard and steer the opposite way
Steer gently in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Take your hands off the wheel
questions.explanation: To recover from a skid, steer gently toward where you want the front of the car to go and avoid sudden braking.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your speed in heavy rain or snow?
choices: Slow down and increase following distance
Speed up to get through quickly
questions.explanation: In rain or snow you should slow down and leave more following distance because traction and visibility are reduced.

questions.prompt: In poor visibility, how fast should you drive?
choices: As fast as the posted limit allows
Slow enough to stop within the distance you can clearly see
Faster to spend less time on the road
questions.explanation: You should always be able to stop within the distance you can see clearly ahead, especially in fog or darkness.

questions.prompt: How far from a fire hydrant must you park in West Virginia?
choices: You may park right next to it
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so that firefighters can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
questions.explanation: Facing downhill, turn your wheels toward the curb so the vehicle rolls into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: In West Virginia, which children must ride in a child safety or booster seat?
choices: Children under 8 who are shorter than 4 feet 9 inches
questions.explanation: West Virginia requires children under 8 and shorter than 4 feet 9 inches to ride in an approved child or booster seat.

questions.prompt: Where is parking prohibited?
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited in crosswalks and intersections because it blocks traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the vehicle rolls back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: What is the single most effective way to protect yourself in a crash?
questions.explanation: Wearing a seat belt is the most effective way to reduce injury or death in a crash.

questions.prompt: After parking on a hill, what else should you do besides turning your wheels?
choices: Set the parking brake and leave the vehicle in park or in gear
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake and leave an automatic in park, or a manual in gear, when parking on a hill.

questions.prompt: Where should you park if your vehicle breaks down on a highway?
choices: Off the road on the shoulder whenever possible
In the middle of the road
questions.explanation: You should never stop on the traveled part of a highway when you can pull off onto the shoulder.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a Level 1 instruction permit in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: You may apply for a Level 1 instruction permit at age 15 after passing the required tests.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must a Level 1 permit holder complete before advancing?
questions.explanation: A Level 1 permit holder must complete 50 hours of supervised driving, including at least 10 hours at night.

questions.prompt: Who must supervise a Level 1 permit holder in West Virginia?
choices: A licensed driver age 21 or older in the front passenger seat
questions.explanation: A Level 1 permit holder must always be supervised by a licensed driver age 21 or older seated in the front.

questions.prompt: How long must a Level 1 instruction permit be held before advancing?
questions.explanation: The Level 1 permit must be held for at least 180 days without certain violations before you can advance.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with a Level 2 intermediate license, what is the night driving restriction?
choices: No driving between 10:00 p.m. and 5:00 a.m. except for specific reasons
No driving after dark at all
questions.explanation: Level 2 drivers may not drive between 10:00 p.m. and 5:00 a.m. except for work, school, or emergencies.

questions.prompt: At what age can a West Virginia driver take the road test for a Level 2 intermediate license?
questions.explanation: At age 16, after meeting permit requirements, you may take the road skills test for a Level 2 intermediate license.

questions.prompt: What passenger restriction applies during the first six months of a Level 2 license?
choices: No non-family passengers under 20, with limited exceptions
questions.explanation: For the first six months, Level 2 drivers may not carry non-family passengers under 20 except in limited situations.

questions.prompt: At what age may a West Virginia driver receive a full Level 3 license?
questions.explanation: At age 17, after meeting all graduated license requirements, a driver may receive a full Level 3 license.

questions.prompt: What does West Virginia use to track unsafe driving behavior?
questions.explanation: West Virginia uses a point system that adds points for moving violations and can lead to suspension.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many points on your driving record?
choices: Your driving privileges can be suspended
You get a new license automatically
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points can result in suspension of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: What documents should you be able to show a law enforcement officer when stopped?
choices: Your license, registration, and proof of insurance
questions.explanation: You must carry and present your license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance when requested.

questions.prompt: What is a likely consequence of a DUI conviction in West Virginia?
choices: License suspension, fines, and possible jail time
questions.explanation: A DUI conviction can lead to license suspension, fines, mandatory education, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: What is the fine for a third handheld phone or texting offense in West Virginia?
questions.explanation: A third handheld phone or texting offense carries a $300 fine plus demerit points on your record.

questions.prompt: Violating West Virginia's Move Over law is classified as what?
choices: A misdemeanor that can bring fines and jail time
questions.explanation: Failing to obey the Move Over law is a misdemeanor that can result in fines and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: For a driver under 21, what BAC level can lead to license suspension?
questions.explanation: Under the zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 faces suspension at a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is treated as a serious driving offense with heavy penalties?
choices: Driving exactly at the speed limit
Leaving the scene of a crash
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash is a serious offense that carries heavy penalties in West Virginia.
